questions.text: Why is it necessary, according to the text, to review the physical culture background in order to understand the topic of fitness?
questions.explanation: According to the text, understanding fitness and the fitness recreation trend requires prior exploration of physical culture background and certain problems of civilizational development.

options.text: Because the rules of sports can only be learned this way
Because understanding the global fitness trend requires historical and conceptual background
Because the text applies exclusively a historical science approach
Because modern sport has no connection to earlier physical culture

questions.text: According to the chapter, the introduction is actually more of a lead-in section than a mere short preface.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that this chapter is more than an introduction — it is intended as a section that leads into the topic.

questions.text: What was one of sport's earliest functions according to the text?
questions.explanation: According to the text, sport initially served as preparation for hunting, and gradually for the warfare of each era.

questions.text: What followed the preparation for hunting in the historical development of sport's function?
questions.explanation: The text clearly indicates the sequence: first hunting, then gradually preparation for warfare.

questions.text: In ancient coming-of-age trials, young people had to prove they were capable of hunting for their livelihood and defending their community.
questions.explanation: According to the text, these trials required the youth to demonstrate their fitness for hunting and for defending their family, tribe, and people.

questions.text: What are sports originally derived from, according to the text?
questions.explanation: According to the authors, sports originally derive from ancient coming-of-age trials.

options.text: The leisure habits of industrial society

questions.text: Which summary best reflects the historical role of sports competitions according to this subsection?
questions.explanation: According to the text, sports competitions and contests throughout human history served as tools for preparation for work and/or warfare.

options.text: They were primarily substitutes for religious ceremonies
They were tools for preparation for work and/or warfare
They always had only an entertainment purpose

questions.text: Which civilizational context is presented in the text as the classical continuation of European physical culture?
questions.explanation: According to the authors, the classical continuation of all this in European culture was in Ancient Greece.

questions.text: According to the text, in Sparta the entire way of life of young men was subordinated to their military training and combat preparation.
questions.explanation: The subsection highlights that in Sparta the entire lifestyle of the youth was subordinated to their military training and combat preparation.

questions.text: Which statement most precisely captures the essence of the passage about Spartan education?
questions.explanation: The core message of the text is that in Sparta, society treated military fitness as a central value and aligned the youths' entire way of life to it.

options.text: The primary goal of education was to develop artistic skills
Military fitness and combat preparation were defining social priorities
Physical education was completely separated from social life
Physical exercise played a role only on festive occasions

questions.text: Based on the text, the modern Olympic Games cannot be interpreted without ancient Greek culture.
questions.explanation: The authors explicitly state that the modern Olympic Games cannot be interpreted without ancient Greek culture.

questions.text: What type of medieval physical activity does the text highlight as serving combat preparation?
questions.explanation: According to the text, in the Middle Ages knightly or chivalric tournaments — serving combat preparation — were popular public festivals.

questions.text: According to the text, playful forms of movement also appeared in medieval festive celebrations.
questions.explanation: The authors clearly indicate that playful forms of movement also appeared in medieval festive celebrations.

questions.text: Which connection does this subsection emphasise most regarding the early history of sport?
questions.explanation: The main message of the whole subsection is that early forms of sport served practical, social and defensive functions — not merely entertainment.

options.text: Early forms of sport were primarily aimed at economic gain
Early forms of sport were closely linked to subsistence, work, and warfare
The development of sport was independent of social needs
Sport served mainly recreational purposes from the very beginning

questions.text: Which answer most accurately summarises the main idea of the 'Physical Culture Background' subsection?
questions.explanation: The subsection shows that the historical roots of sport are to be found in hunting, combat preparation, coming-of-age trials, and socially determined movement culture.

options.text: The history of sport essentially begins with the emergence of modern competitive sport
The historical roots of sport are linked to survival, defence, community fitness, and social functions
The development of physical culture was shaped exclusively by the Olympic movement
Sport fulfilled the same function in every era
